summary: Russian forces have destroyed Ukrainian unmanned boats using FPV drones near Tendrovskaya Spit and the Dnieper River mouth. The "Rubicon" Center reported over 20,000 enemy target eliminations, with 35.4% being unmanned systems, and conducted 132 FPV anti-drone strikes against Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs on March 13, 2026, followed by 136 more on March 14. Ukrainian forces are deploying AI-powered HX-2 drones from Helsing and using FPV tactics such as throwing riders off horses before striking, while Russian troops have engaged in close-range duels with Ukrainian FPVs, including a soldier charging with a bayonet. Ukrainian FPV pilots are also targeting enemy FPV drones and Mavics, with thermal and visual AI systems being used to track and engage targets.
